revision.text: #redirect[[en:Ōtomo_Muroya]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''大伴室屋'''（おおとものむろや、生没年不詳）は、5世紀後半の豪族。父は大伴武以（たけもち）。[[大伴金村]]の祖父とされる。
[[雄略天皇]]から[[武烈天皇]]まで5代の天皇に仕え、大連（おおむらじ）に任じられた。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代に衣通郎女（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定めている。[[雄略天皇]]の没後には星川皇子の乱を平定、[[清寧天皇]]（允恭天皇の皇孫）の代には諸国に白髪部の舎人・膳夫・靫負を置いた。武烈天皇の代には、城を水派邑に築城している。
「[[日本三代実録]]」「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では、佐伯直・佐伯首・佐伯宿禰の祖としている。
{{stub}} [[Category:飛鳥時代|おおとものむろや]] [[Category:大伴氏|おおとものむろや]]

[[雄略天皇]]から[[武烈天皇]]まで5代の天皇に仕え、[[大連]]（おおむらじ）に任じられた。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代に[[衣通郎女]]（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定めている。雄略天皇の没後には星川皇子の乱を平定、[[清寧天皇]]（允恭天皇の皇孫）の代には諸国に白髪部の舎人・膳夫・靫負を置いた。武烈天皇の代には、城を水派邑に築城している。
「[[日本三代実録]]」「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では、佐伯直・佐伯首・佐伯宿禰の祖としている。

'''大伴室屋'''（おおとものむろや、生没年不詳）は、5世紀後半の豪族。父は[[大伴武以]]（たけもち）とされるが、世代が合わず不審。また、[[大伴金村]]の祖父（一説に父）で、[[大伴談]]の父（一説に兄）とされる。
[[雄略天皇]]から[[武烈天皇]]まで5代の天皇に仕え、[[大連]]（おおむらじ）に任じられた。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代に[[衣通郎女]]（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定めている。雄略天皇の没後には星川皇子の乱を平定、[[清寧天皇]]（允恭天皇の皇孫）の代には諸国に白髪部の舎人・膳夫・靫負を置いた。武烈天皇の代には、城を水派邑に築城している（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべきか）。
「[[日本三代実録]]」「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では、佐伯直・佐伯首・佐伯宿禰の祖としている。

revision.comment: link先変更
revision.text:
'''大伴室屋'''（おおとものむろや、生没年不詳）は、5世紀後半の豪族。父は[[大伴武以]]（たけもち）とされるが、世代が合わず不審。また、[[大伴金村]]の祖父（一説に父）で、[[大伴談]]の父（一説に兄）とされる。
[[雄略天皇]]から[[武烈天皇]]まで5代の天皇に仕え、[[連|大連]]（おおむらじ）に任じられた。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代に[[衣通郎女]]（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定めている。雄略天皇の没後には星川皇子の乱を平定、[[清寧天皇]]（允恭天皇の皇孫）の代には諸国に白髪部の舎人・膳夫・靫負を置いた。武烈天皇の代には、城を水派邑に築城している（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべきか）。
「[[日本三代実録]]」「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では、佐伯直・佐伯首・佐伯宿禰の祖としている。
{{People-stub}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|おおとものむろや]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]]

'''大伴室屋'''（おおとものむろや、生没年不詳）は、[[5世紀]]後半の豪族。父は[[大伴武以]]（たけもち）とされるが（『[[日本三代実録]]』）、世代が合わないので不審。また、[[大伴談]]の父（一説に兄）、[[大伴金村]]の祖父とされる。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖（『[[新撰姓氏録]]』）。
[[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[連|大連]]（おおむらじ）として仕えた（『[[公卿補任]]』）。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年、（[[458年]]）[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、来目部に命じて処刑させる。同23年（[[479年]]）、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年（[[481年]]）には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部（しなべ）、部曲・民部（かきべ）、子代（こしろ）・名代（なしろ）・御名代（みなしろ）|御名代]]として白髪部[[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年（[[501年]]）天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。
{{People-stub}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|おおとものむろや]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]]

'''大伴室屋'''（おおとものむろや、生没年不詳）は、[[5世紀]]後半の豪族。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]（むらじ）。父は[[大伴武以]]（たけもち）とされるが（『[[公卿補任]]』）、世代が合わないので不審。また、[[大伴談]]の父（一説に兄）、[[大伴金村]]の祖父とされる。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖（『[[新撰姓氏録]]』）。
[[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[連|大連]]（おおむらじ）として仕えた（『[[公卿補任]]』）。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年（[[458年]]）、[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、来目部に命じて処刑させる。同23年（[[479年]]）8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年（[[481年]]）には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部（しなべ）、部曲・民部（かきべ）、子代（こしろ）・名代（なしろ）・御名代（みなしろ）|御名代]]として白髪部[[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年（[[501年]]）天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。
{{People-stub}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|おおとものむろや]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]]

revision.comment: [[大連 (古代日本)|]]
revision.text:
'''大伴室屋'''（おおとものむろや、生没年不詳）は、[[5世紀]]後半の豪族。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]（むらじ）。父は[[大伴武以]]（たけもち）とされるが（『[[公卿補任]]』）、世代が合わないので不審。また、[[大伴談]]の父（一説に兄）、[[大伴金村]]の祖父とされる。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖（『[[新撰姓氏録]]』）。
[[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]（おおむらじ）として仕えた（『[[公卿補任]]』）。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年（[[458年]]）、[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、来目部に命じて処刑させる。同23年（[[479年]]）8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年（[[481年]]）には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部（しなべ）、部曲・民部（かきべ）、子代（こしろ）・名代（なしろ）・御名代（みなしろ）|御名代]]として白髪部[[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年（[[501年]]）天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。
{{People-stub}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|おおとものむろや]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]]

'''大伴 室屋'''（おおとも の むろや、生没年不詳）は、[[5世紀]]後半の豪族。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]（むらじ）。父は[[大伴武以]]（たけもち）とされるが（『[[公卿補任]]』）、世代が合わないので不審。また、[[大伴談]]の父（一説に兄）、[[大伴金村]]の祖父とされる。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖（『[[新撰姓氏録]]』）。
[[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]（おおむらじ）として仕えた（『[[公卿補任]]』）。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の皇子）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]（そとおりのいらつめ）のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年（[[458年]]）、[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、来目部に命じて処刑させる。同23年（[[479年]]）8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年（[[481年]]）には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部（しなべ）、部曲・民部（かきべ）、子代（こしろ）・名代（なしろ）・御名代（みなしろ）|御名代]]として白髪部[[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年（[[501年]]）天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。

image
[[category:古墳時代の人物]] -> [[category:日本神話の人物]]：武烈天皇紀以前に記載される非実在性の強い人物につき（武烈天皇紀以前とする根拠は日本人名大辞典による）。

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大伴 室屋 | 画像 = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | 画像サイズ = 200px | 画像説明 = 大伴室屋（[[菊池容斎]]『前賢故実』 | 時代 = [[古墳時代]]中期 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大連 (古代日本)|大連]] | 主君 = [[允恭天皇]] → [[安康天皇]] → [[雄略天皇]] →
[[清寧天皇]] → [[顕宗天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴]][[連]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = あり | 子 = あり | 特記事項 = [[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[連]]、[[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[宿禰]]祖。 }} '''大伴 室屋'''（おおとも の むろや、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[5世紀]]後半の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。一般に流布している系図では[[大伴武以]]（武持・健持：たけもち）の子とされるが「伴氏系図」（『[[続群書類従]]』巻第182所収）等、世代が合わない武以は[[仲哀天皇]]代の人物とされる（『[[日本書紀]]』仲哀天皇9年2月5日条）。ほかに、武以と室屋の間に2-3代を記す系図もある『伴党水党並甲賀侍由緒書』（内閣文庫所蔵）では佐彦・山前の二代を、『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻1142,山岡氏では健友・武季・建室の三代を記す。。子に[[大伴談|談]]・御物がいたとする系図がある『古屋家家譜』（『甲斐国一之宮浅間神社誌』資料編及び『各家系譜』所収）によると、室屋の弟に談を、子に[[大伴金村|金村]]・御物・若古を置く。。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京神別中 == 経歴 == [[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]として仕えた『[[公卿補任]]』。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の[[皇子]]）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年（[[458年]]）、[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、来目部に命じて処刑させる。同23年（[[479年]]）8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川稚宮皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年（[[481年]]）には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部、部曲・民部、子代・名代・御名代|御名代]]として白髪部[[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年（[[501年]]）天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、現在の[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。 == 参考文献 == * [[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』（古代氏族研究会、1986年） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{people-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の むろや}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]] [[Category:大和国の人物]]

revision.comment: [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] -> [[Category:日本神話の人物]]：理由は先に同じ。表記ガイドに則り武烈天皇以前の西暦も除去。
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大伴 室屋 | 画像 = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | 画像サイズ = 200px | 画像説明 = 大伴室屋（[[菊池容斎]]『前賢故実』 | 時代 = | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大連 (古代日本)|大連]] | 主君 = [[允恭天皇]] → [[安康天皇]] → [[雄略天皇]] →
[[清寧天皇]] → [[顕宗天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴]][[連]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = あり | 子 = あり | 特記事項 = [[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[連]]、[[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[宿禰]]祖。 }} '''大伴 室屋'''（おおとも の むろや、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代日本の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。一般に流布している系図では[[大伴武以]]（武持・健持：たけもち）の子とされるが「伴氏系図」（『[[続群書類従]]』巻第182所収）等、世代は合わない武以は[[仲哀天皇]]代の人物とされる（『[[日本書紀]]』仲哀天皇9年2月5日条）。ほかに、武以と室屋の間に2-3代を記す系図もある『伴党水党並甲賀侍由緒書』（内閣文庫所蔵）では佐彦・山前の二代を、『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻1142,山岡氏では健友・武季・建室の三代を記す。。子に[[大伴談|談]]・御物がいたとする系図がある『古屋家家譜』（『甲斐国一之宮浅間神社誌』資料編及び『各家系譜』所収）によると、室屋の弟に談を、子に[[大伴金村|金村]]・御物・若古を置く。。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京神別中 == 経歴 == [[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]として仕えた『[[公卿補任]]』。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の[[皇子]]）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年、[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、来目部に命じて処刑させる。同23年8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川稚宮皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部、部曲・民部、子代・名代・御名代|御名代]]として白髪部[[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、現在の[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。 == 参考文献 == * [[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』（古代氏族研究会、1986年） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{people-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の むろや}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]] [[Category:大和国の人物]]

[[清寧天皇]] → [[顕宗天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴]][[連]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = あり | 子 = あり | 特記事項 = [[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[連]]、[[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[宿禰]]祖。 }} '''大伴 室屋'''（おおとも の むろや、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代日本の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。一般に流布している系図では[[大伴武以]]（武持・健持：たけもち）の子とされるが「伴氏系図」（『[[続群書類従]]』巻第182所収）等、世代は合わない武以は[[仲哀天皇]]代の人物とされる（『[[日本書紀]]』仲哀天皇9年2月5日条）。ほかに、武以と室屋の間に2-3代を記す系図もある『伴党水党並甲賀侍由緒書』（内閣文庫所蔵）では佐彦・山前の二代を、『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻1142,山岡氏では健友・武季・建室の三代を記す。。子に[[大伴談|談]]・御物がいたとする系図がある『古屋家家譜』（『甲斐国一之宮浅間神社誌』資料編及び『各家系譜』所収）によると、室屋の弟に談を、子に[[大伴金村|金村]]・御物・若古を置く。。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京神別中。 == 経歴 == [[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]として仕えた『[[公卿補任]]』。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の[[皇子]]）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年、[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、[[久米部|来目部]]に命じて処刑させる。同23年8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川稚宮皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部、部曲・民部、子代・名代・御名代|御名代]]として[[白髪部]][[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、現在の[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。 == 参考文献 == * [[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』（古代氏族研究会、1986年） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{people-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の むろや}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]] [[Category:大和国の人物]]

[[清寧天皇]] → [[顕宗天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴連]] | 父母 = [[大伴武以]]または[[大伴山前]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = あり | 子 = [[大伴談|談連]]、若子連、一説：[[大伴金村|金村連]]、御物連 | 特記事項 = [[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[連]]、[[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[宿禰]]祖。 }} '''大伴 室屋'''（おおとも の むろや、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代日本の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。一般に流布している系図では[[大伴武以]]（武持・健持：たけもち）の子とされるが「伴氏系図」（『[[続群書類従]]』巻第182所収）等、世代は合わない武以は[[仲哀天皇]]代の人物とされる（『[[日本書紀]]』仲哀天皇9年2月5日条）。ほかに、武以と室屋の間に2-3代を記す系図もある『伴党水党並甲賀侍由緒書』（内閣文庫所蔵）では佐彦・山前の二代を、『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻1142,山岡氏では健友・武季・建室の三代を記す。。子に[[大伴談|談]]・御物がいたとする系図がある『古屋家家譜』（『甲斐国一之宮浅間神社誌』資料編及び『各家系譜』所収）によると、室屋の弟に談を、子に[[大伴金村|金村]]・御物・若古を置く。。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京神別中。 == 経歴 == [[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]として仕えた『[[公卿補任]]』。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の[[皇子]]）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年、[[百済]]の池津媛を犯した石川楯を、[[久米部|来目部]]に命じて処刑させる。同23年8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川稚宮皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#品部、部曲・民部、子代・名代・御名代|御名代]]として[[白髪部]][[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、現在の[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた（ただし、これは金村の事績とすべき）。 == 参考文献 == * [[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』（古代氏族研究会、1986年） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{people-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の むろや}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大伴 室屋 | 画像 = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | 画像サイズ = 200px | 画像説明 = 大伴室屋（[[菊池容斎]]画『[[前賢故実]]』 | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[大連 (古代日本)|大連]] | 主君 = [[允恭天皇]] → [[安康天皇]] → [[雄略天皇]] →
[[清寧天皇]] → [[顕宗天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴連]] | 父母 = [[大伴武以]]？ | 兄弟 = | 妻 = あり | 子 = [[大伴談|談連]]、若子連、一説：[[大伴金村|金村連]]、御物連 | 特記事項 = [[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[連]]、[[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[宿禰]]祖。 }} '''大伴 室屋'''（おおとも の むろや、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代日本の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。一般に流布している系図では[[大伴武以]]（武持・健持：たけもち）の子とされるが「伴氏系図」（『[[続群書類従]]』巻第182所収）等、世代は合わない武以は[[仲哀天皇]]代の人物とされる（『[[日本書紀]]』仲哀天皇9年2月5日条）。ほかに、武以と室屋の間に3代を記す系図もある『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻1142,山岡氏では健友・武季・建室の三代を記す。。子に[[大伴談|談]]・御物がいたとする系図がある『古屋家家譜』（『甲斐国一之宮浅間神社誌』資料編及び『各家系譜』所収）によると、室屋の弟に談を、子に[[大伴金村|金村]]・御物・若古を置く。。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京神別中。 == 経歴 == [[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]として仕えた『[[公卿補任]]』。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の[[皇子]]）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年、[[百済]]の[[池津媛]]を犯した[[石川楯]]を、[[久米部|来目部]]に命じて処刑させる。同23年8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川稚宮皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#分類|御名代]]として[[白髪部]][[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、現在の[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』（古代氏族研究会、1986年） == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{people-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の むろや}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

[[清寧天皇]] → [[顕宗天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴連]] | 父母 = [[大伴山前]]？ | 兄弟 = | 妻 = あり | 子 = [[大伴談|談連]]、若子連、一説：[[大伴金村|金村連]]、御物連 | 特記事項 = [[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[連]]、[[佐伯氏|佐伯]][[宿禰]]祖。 }} '''大伴 室屋'''（おおとも の むろや、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代日本の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。一般に流布している系図では[[大伴武以]]（武持・健持：たけもち）の子とされるが「伴氏系図」（『[[続群書類従]]』巻第182所収）等、世代は合わない武以は[[仲哀天皇]]代の人物とされる（『[[日本書紀]]』仲哀天皇9年2月5日条）。ほかに、武以と室屋の間に3代を記す系図もある『[[寛政重修諸家譜]]』巻1142,山岡氏では健友・武季・建室の三代を記す。。子に[[大伴談|談]]・御物がいたとする系図がある『古屋家家譜』（『甲斐国一之宮浅間神社誌』資料編及び『各家系譜』所収）によると、室屋の弟に談を、子に[[大伴金村|金村]]・御物・若古を置く。。[[佐伯氏|佐伯連・佐伯宿禰]]の祖『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京神別中。 == 経歴 == [[允恭天皇]]から[[顕宗天皇]]まで5代の天皇に[[大連 (古代日本)|大連]]として仕えた『[[公卿補任]]』。[[允恭天皇]]（[[仁徳天皇]]の[[皇子]]）の代、妃の[[衣通姫|衣通郎姫]]のために藤原部を定める。[[雄略天皇]]2年、[[百済]]の[[池津媛]]を犯した[[石川楯]]を、[[久米部|来目部]]に命じて処刑させる。同23年8月、天皇[[崩御]]に際して後事を託され、直後に起こった[[星川稚宮皇子]]の叛乱を[[東漢掬]]と共に鎮圧。[[清寧天皇]]2年には、諸国に天皇の[[部民制#分類|御名代]]として[[白髪部]][[舎人]]・[[膳夫]]（かしわで）・[[靱負]]（ゆげい）を置いた。[[武烈天皇]]3年天皇の[[詔]]に従い、役丁を徴発して城の形を水派邑（みまたのむら、現在の[[奈良県]][[河合町]]か）に築いた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』（古代氏族研究会、1986年） == 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{people-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の むろや}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:大伴氏|むろや]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[ja:大伴室屋]]

{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Muroya}} [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:大伴室屋]]

In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo revision.comment: Adding [[Category:ファイル]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo Muroya}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:kk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōtomo revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ōtomo revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People
