{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People revision.comment: Removing this excess
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Deprecated qq open]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōtomo revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Long o change]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo [[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtomo revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | Image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | Image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People
