{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:suQ11q]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo Muroya}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo Muroya}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}}

During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Muroya}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo Muroya}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo Muroya}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]]

[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Ōomi]] (Great Minister). During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:大伴氏]]

Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province�]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Muroya]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:People of the Kofun period]] [[Category:People from Yamato Province]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[Zoku Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[Kōryō]], [[Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]]

[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]]

[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[sn:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}}

His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}}

revision.text:
[[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Otomo no Muroya.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Katari|WD=Q11432354}}|father={{ill|Q97819086|WD=Q97819086}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|native_name=大伴室屋}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432336]] Wikidata name: Muroya Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] }} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}}

Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

[[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Otomo no Muroya.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Katari|WD=Q11432354}}|father={{ill|Q97819086|WD=Q97819086}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|native_name=大伴室屋}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432336]] Wikidata name: Muroya Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] → [[Emperor Ankō]] → [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] →
[[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]] → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''[[:en:Gunsho Ruijū|Gunsho Ruijū]]'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]],'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] (a son of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of [[:en:Emperor Seinei|Emperor Seinei]], he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]], he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

revision.text:
[[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:大伴室屋]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Otomo no Muroya.jpg|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Katari|WD=Q11432354}}|father={{ill|Q97819086|WD=Q97819086}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|native_name=大伴室屋}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432336]] Wikidata name: Muroya Ootomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Noble | Name = Ōtomo no Muroya | image = Otomo no Muroya.jpg | image size = 200px | Caption = Ōtomo no Muroya (illustration by [[Kikuchi Yōsai]] from ''Zenken Kojitsu'') | Period = [[Kofun period]] | Birth Date = Unknown | Death Date = Unknown | Former Name = | Other Names = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Buddhist Name = | Burial Place = | Court Rank = [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister) | Allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō}} → [[Emperor Ankō]] → {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}} →
{{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|WD=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei}} → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''{{ill|Gunsho Ruijū|en|Gunsho Ruijū|WD=Q1554852|lt=Gunsho Ruijū}}'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}},'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō}} to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō}} (a son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}, Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of {{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|WD=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei}}, he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}}, he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

{{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|WD=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei|1=Emperor Seinei}} → [[Emperor Kenzei]] | Clan = [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo no Muraji]] | Parents = Father: [[Ōtomo no Yamamae]]? | Siblings = | Spouse = Married | Children = [[Ōtomo no Tan|Tan no Muraji]], Wakako no Muraji; possibly [[Ōtomo no Kanamura|Kanamura no Muraji]], Mimono no Muraji | Special Notes = Ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]] (Saeki no Muraji and Saeki no Sukune). }} '''Ōtomo no Muroya''' (大伴 室屋, dates unknown) was a powerful noble of ancient Japan during the [[Kofun period]]. His title was [[Muraji]] (連). While widely accepted genealogies trace him as the son of [[Ōtomo no Takei]] (武持),"Banshi Keizu" (recorded in ''{{ill|Gunsho Ruijū|en|Gunsho Ruijū|WD=Q1554852|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|1=Gunsho Ruijū}}'' Vol. 182). this generational link appears inconsistent.Ōtomo no Takei is said to have lived during {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}'s era (''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' Chūai 9th year, 2nd month, 5th day). Some genealogies add three generations between Takei and Muroya.The ''Kan'ei Shokukei Sanpitsu'' notes the intermediaries Takei, Musuki, and Tateya. His children are recorded as Tan and Mimono, with some genealogies adding Kanamura and Wakako.''Furuya-ke Keifu'' ("Genealogy of the Furuya Clan"), as cited in the ''Kai no Kuni Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja-shi'' and ''Various Family Genealogies.'' Muroya is considered an ancestor of the [[Saeki Clan]].''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}},'' Left Capital Divine Ancestry Section. == Career == Ōtomo no Muroya served five emperors from {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō|1=Emperor Ingyō}} to [[Emperor Kenzei]] as [[Omi (title)|Ōomi]] (Great Minister). Under {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō|1=Emperor Ingyō}} (a son of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}), he established the Fujiwara Division for [[Otoyorihime]]. In the second year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, Muroya ordered the execution of [[Ishikawa Tateno]] for assaulting the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] emissary Iketsuhime, using the [[Kumebe]] to carry out the punishment. In the 23rd year of Yūryaku’s reign, after the emperor's death, Muroya was entrusted with state affairs and suppressed a rebellion by [[Hoshikawa no Wakamiyanomiko]] alongside [[Azumahito no Sukuri]]. During the second year of {{ill|Emperor Seinei|en|Emperor Seinei|WD=Q357273|lt=Emperor Seinei|1=Emperor Seinei}}, he introduced imperial subjects (御名代), including the [[Shirakabe-be]], [[Toneri]], [[:en:Kashiwade|Kashiwade]] (provisioners), and [[Yugei]] (bowmen), across the provinces. In the third year of {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}}, he led the conscription of workers to construct a fortified area in Mimata Village (modern-day {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō, Nara|1=Kōryō, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) following imperial orders. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * [[Toshio Hōga]]: ''Ancient Clan Genealogy Compilation'' (古代氏族系譜集成, Kodai Shizoku Keifu Shūsei), Kodai Shizoku Kenkyūkai, 1986. == See Also == * [[List of People Before the Asuka Period]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|大伴室屋|version=104410414|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴室屋]] [[ja:Ōtomo Muroya]] {{wikidata link|Q11432336}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan (ancient Japan)]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:pages]]
